meaning: n.扑通声,坠落adv.扑通一声地vt.使掉下vi.扑通落下
examples: Can you just plop some ice in my drink?
Another drop of water fell with a soft plop.
I look forward to seeing Julie smile when I plop the gifts on her desk.
Young people, often with kids in tow, plop on chairs to watch videos on their smartphones.
Lawrence's agency, Wells Rich Greene, created some of the most memorable commercials, including "Plop-plop, fizz-fizz" (for Alka-Seltzer).
